question: How does Xinpinmu specifically solve Temu's tariff problem, and what does the full value chain look like?

findings.claim: EU Council agreed to impose EUR 3 flat customs duty on all parcels under EUR 150, effective July 1, 2026
findings.data: On December 12, 2025, the European Council formally agreed to a fixed EUR 3 customs duty per item category on small parcels entering the EU from July 1, 2026. The duty applies to each distinct tariff sub-heading in a parcel (e.g., silk blouse + wool blouse = EUR 6). Covers IOSS-registered non-EU sellers who account for 93% of cross-border e-commerce imports.

findings.claim: EU will add an additional EUR 2 handling fee per consignment starting November 2026, and EUR 0.50 for warehouse-routed parcels
findings.data: European Commission proposed EUR 2 handling fee for direct-to-consumer parcels from outside the EU, and EUR 0.50 for parcels first routed to EU warehouses before shipping to end consumers. This creates a direct financial incentive for the warehouse-first (bulk import) model. Combined impact: EUR 3 duty + EUR 2 handling fee = EUR 5 per parcel for direct cross-border; EUR 3 duty + EUR 0.50 = EUR 3.50 for warehouse-routed.

findings.claim: EUR 3 flat duty is temporary; from 2028 all imports under EUR 150 will face standard tariff-based duties when EU Customs Data Hub launches
findings.data: The EUR 3 duty is explicitly temporary (July 2026 - July 2028, extendable). When the EU Customs Data Hub becomes operational, the EUR 150 threshold disappears entirely and all imports will be subject to standard product-level tariff rates. EU standard tariff rates on Chinese goods range from 10-20% depending on category.

findings.claim: EUR 3 flat duty destroys margins on ultra-low-price items (EUR 20 product example: profit wiped out)
findings.data: A EUR 20 trinket with ~15% margin (EUR 3 profit) loses all profit after EUR 2 handling fee + EUR 2+ customs duty. 91% of small shipments into the EU originate from China. 4.6 billion such packages entered the EU in 2024, nearly double 2023 figures. 65% of parcels are estimated to be undervalued to fall below the EUR 150 line.

findings.claim: EU regulatory design explicitly incentivizes warehouse-first model over direct cross-border shipping
findings.data: The EUR 2 direct-to-consumer handling fee vs EUR 0.50 warehouse-routed fee creates a EUR 1.50/parcel cost advantage for the bulk-import-to-warehouse model. This regulatory design directly rewards the Xinpinmu-type value chain (factory -> bulk ship -> EU warehouse -> local delivery) over the old Temu model (factory -> air parcel -> direct to consumer).
